section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports experiencing dizziness and lightheadedness, particularly when sitting.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Chief Complaint should contain a concise statement of the primary reason for visit (e.g., 'dizziness and lightheadedness'), not a full narrative sentence with context such as 'particularly when sitting.' The content itself is appropriate to HPI, though this does not substantially mislead the reader.

section_miscategorizations.text: He reports his knee replacement is functioning well and he is walking without problems.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is more of a functional status/physical finding than part of the current illness narrative. However, including it in HPI as contextual history is not seriously misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: He reports having some tenderness to the right lower quadrant upon abdominal palpation.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Tenderness on palpation is an examination finding rather than a purely patient-reported symptom. It would more appropriately belong in Findings, but its presence in HPI does not substantially distort clinical meaning.
